@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "nats-4121-ability-to-reset-consumer-start-sequence",
+  "missionClass": "fixer",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "nats: Ability to reset consumer start sequence",
+    "description": "Ability to reset consumer start sequence. Requested by 5+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current nats deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your nats version and configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats\nhelm list -n nats 2>/dev/null || echo \"Not installed via Helm\"\n```\nThis feature requires a working nats installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review nats configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant nats configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats\nkubectl get configmap -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=nats\n```\n## Feature Request\n\n#### Use Case:\n\nThe motivation comes from event sourcing-style use cases, but can apply to general _sourcing_ of a stream into some materialized form."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Ability to reset consumer start sequence",
+        "description": "Currently we don't offer durable ordered consumption of a stream. We do support ephemeral ordered push consumers, but those can be problematic when using those on WQ/Interest streams as as soon as the server sends the message the \"ack\" happens immediately, resulting in the message to be removed. Similarly recreating the consumer (through delete+create) may lose interest and remove messages. Durable consumers could be used for guaranteed ordered consumption by using the \"pinning\" introduced in\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n nats -l app.kubernetes.io/name=nats\nkubectl get events -n nats --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Ability to reset consumer start sequence\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "Currently we don't offer durable ordered consumption of a stream. We do support ephemeral ordered push consumers, but those can be problematic when using those on WQ/Interest streams as as soon as the server sends the message the \"ack\" happens immediately, resulting in the message to be removed. Similarly recreating the consumer (through delete+create) may lose interest and remove messages.",
+      "codeSnippets": []
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "nats",
+      "incubating",
+      "networking",
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "nats"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "intermediate",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "incubating",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/nats-io/nats-server/issues/4121",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/nats-io/nats-server",
+      "pr": "https://github.com/nats-io/nats-server/pull/7489"
+    },
+    "reactions": 5,
+    "comments": 23,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "kubernetes": ">=1.24",
+    "tools": [
+      "kubectl"
+    ],
+    "description": "A running Kubernetes cluster with nats installed or the issue environment reproducible."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-04-01T06:46:39.287Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}

+        "description": "Currently we don't offer durable ordered consumption of a stream.
